summary.intent: Optimize the `saveGitOidMapping` function to avoid redundant DB reads on every index pass by pre-fetching already-mapped checkpoints upfront.
summary.outcome: Implemented a two-phase optimization: first pre-fetching mapped checkpoints into a Set (then Map) before the loop, then refining to use a Map<checkpointId, gitOid> to also handle rebase scenarios where the same checkpoint ID maps to a new git OID.

repo: The `gitOid` field is `@id` in the Prisma schema, meaning it's the primary key for the git OID mapping table â€” upsert/update logic must account for this.
The indexer uses `indexAllCheckpointsV2` as the top-level loop and `indexCheckpointV2` as the per-checkpoint handler in `/Users/stephanostsoucas/gossamer/src/indexer.ts`.

Checkpoint-to-git-OID mappings can change after a rebase, so a checkpoint ID alone is not sufficient to determine if a mapping is still valid â€” both checkpointId and gitOid must be checked together.

code.finding: `indexCheckpointV2` was updated to accept an optional `mappedCheckpointIds` parameter (first a Set<string>, then refactored to Map<string, string>) to skip or conditionally run `saveGitOidMapping`.
`indexAllCheckpointsV2` pre-fetches all existing checkpoint-to-gitOid mappings with a single `findMany` query before the loop, passing the result into each `indexCheckpointV2` call.

`saveGitOidMapping` now owns the skip/upsert logic: it resolves the commit OID first, compares against the pre-fetched map, skips if the exact (checkpointId, gitOid) pair already exists, and upserts if the OID changed.

workflow: Running `npm run build` to verify TypeScript compilation after refactoring is a standard validation step in this project.

When pre-fetching data to skip redundant work, consider all dimensions of uniqueness â€” not just the primary lookup key â€” especially when external state (like git history) can change independently.

friction: Initial optimization used only checkpointId as the cache key (Set<string>), which was incorrect for rebase scenarios â€” required a second round of edits to switch to Map<checkpointId, gitOid>.
open_items: The upsert behavior in `saveGitOidMapping` when a OID changes (post-rebase) should be tested explicitly to confirm it correctly replaces the old mapping given `gitOid` is the `@id` primary key.
The in-memory map is updated after a successful new mapping but behavior on upsert (OID change) path may need verification that the map stays consistent within a pass.
Consider whether the pre-fetched map should be scoped per-repository/branch if multiple repos are indexed in the same pass.
